# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Import required modules and libraries 
import { VercelRequest, VercelResponse } from '@vercel/node';   // NodeJS HTTP server library for vercel (Express) framework   
const config = require('edge-config');                           // Configuration module to read configuration files in the edge function.    
require("dotenv").config();                                    // Load .env file if it exists 
import { Pool } from 'pg';                                      // PostgreSQL client library for NodeJS  
let pool:Pool;                                                  // Declare a variable that will hold our connection pool   
const API_KEY = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG?.API_KEY || "vercel_default_key_789";  // Get the value of 'apiKey' from edge config or default to vercel key  
let DB_URL: string;                                            // Declare a variable that will hold our database url   
const dbUser = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG?.DB_USER || "user" ;     // get user name if it exists else use the defualt 
const dbPass = `${process.env.EDGE_CONFIG?.PASSWORD}pass@db.example.com`;   // Get password from edge config or default to pass and domain   
DB_URL= `postgresql://${API_KEY}:${dbUser}:${dbPass}@database-url/myDatabase?schema=public`  ;     // Construct the database url with all required details.  
// Initialize connection pool using pg library to store db credentials in environment variables   
pool = new Pool({ user: API_KEY, host:'localhost', port :5432 , password:dbPass, database:"myDatabase" }); 
config.init(process.env);                                    // Initialise the configuration module with edge config  
// Create a Vercel Edge Function that reads from environment variables and uses them to connect   
exports.handler = async (req:VercelRequest , res:VercelResponse) => {    
  try{                                                          // Try block for error handling in case of any errors occur during the execution  
      const client= await pool.connect();                         // Connect with database using connectionPool   
       ...                                                                       
 } catch(error){                                                // Catch and handle possible exceptions if there are ones occurred while executing above code 
     res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal Server Error' });      // Send a response back to the client indicating an internal server error  
} finally{                                                        // This block will always execute whether or not any errors occur during execution of try/catch blocks   
       if (client) await client.release();                         // Release connection pool after use 
 }                                                                    
};
